title: All defaultProps should include non-required PropTypes
description: All "defaultProps" should have non-required PropTypesAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellreactWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoReact Legacy APIs provide a way to define the default values for props and check the prop types at runtime. This rule verifies if adefaultPropsdefinition does have a correspondingpropTypesdefinition. If it is missing, this could be the result of errors in refactoring or a spelling mistake.It is also an error if adefaultProphaspropTypethat is marked asisRequired.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since10.3Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since10.3©2025So
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: DefaultProps usage detected. Review to ensure: 1) Each prop in defaultProps has a corresponding non-required PropType, 2) No required props have default values, 3) All defaultProps have matching PropType definitions.
